perturbation.original_text: Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein. However, Mellet may develop products which do not in any material way compete with any Product of Sweeetskinz so long as such development is of no cost to Sweetskinz, is done after “working hours” (defined as 45-hour work week) and does not interfere with Mellet’s responsibilities as the Chief Technology Officer of Sweetskinz.
perturbation.changed_text: Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein. However, Mellet may develop products which do not in any material way compete with any Product of Sweeetskinz so long as such development is of no cost to Sweetskinz, is done after “working hours” and does not interfere with Mellet’s responsibilities as the Chief Technology Officer of Sweetskinz.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'working hours' as a 45-hour work week, the passage becomes vague about what constitutes 'after working hours', contradicting Mellet's responsibilities as Cheif Technology Officer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: any idea, concept, technique, invention, creation, copyrightable or patentable work ("Product") relating to pneumatic colorized tires or other products sold or developed by Sweetskinz, that Mellet is instrumental in creating, developing or producing, or helping to create, develop or produce, during the time that Mellet is employed by Sweetskinz shall be considered owned by Sweetskinz, whether conceived by Mellet individually or jointly, on or off Sweetskinz's premises. Mellet hereby irrevocably assigns such Product to Sweetskinz and authorizes Sweetskinz to execute on Mellet’s behalf such documents as it may deem necessary to confirm and protect its interests therein. However, Mellet may develop products which do not in any material way compete with any Product of Sweeetskinz so long as such development is of no cost to Sweetskinz, is done after “working hours” and does not interfere with Mellet’s responsibilities as the Chief Technology Officer of Sweetskinz. Mellet acknowledges that this Section 2 is reasonable, among other things, because Sweetskinz anticipates selling its products on all continents of the world and developing relationships with manufacturers, wholesalers and retailers on such continents
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'working hours' as a 45-hour work week. The changed text omits this definition. This omission introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'after working hours'. This ambiguity contradicts the earlier part of the contradicted text because it is no longer clear when Mellet's product development is considered outside of their Sweetskinz employment and thus potentially owned by Mellet, as the boundary of 'working hours' is no longer defined, which would cause a strong internal inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees at all times during the Term to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees at all times during to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'the Term' creates uncertainty about when the confidentiality obligation begins and ends. Since 'Term' has already been altered, deleting it here makes it even more ambiguous and contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIALITY: Each Party agrees at all times during to hold in the strictest confidence, and not use, except for the benefit of the Parties or to disclose, transfer or reveal, directly or indirectly to any person or entity any confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Each Party agrees at all times during the Term...", but the changed text omits "the Term". The contradicted text also omits "the Term", which means the original contradiction is recreated. The explanation states that removing 'the Term' creates uncertainty about when the confidentiality obligation begins and ends, making it ambiguous and contradictory, thus the change does cause a strong in-text contradiction.
